drug_interactions.effect_description: Reduced lithium clearance causes lithium toxicity
drug_interactions.management: Avoid concurrent use or reduce lithium dose by 50% and monitor levels closely
drug_interactions.mechanism: Thiazides decrease lithium renal clearance by 20-40%

drug_interactions.management: Monitor K+ closely; maintain K+ > 4.0 mmol/L; may need K+ supplementation
drug_interactions.mechanism: Low K+ enhances digoxin binding to Na+/K+-ATPase

drug_interactions.effect_description: Reduced diuretic efficacy; increased renal risk
drug_interactions.management: Monitor renal function; consider alternative analgesics
Monitor K+ closely; consider K+ supplementation prophylactically
Monitor K+ if high-dose beta-agonist therapy
drug_interactions.mechanism: Both stimulate K+ shift into cells

evidence.text: Hypokalemia (serum K+ < 3.5 mmol/L) occurs in 5-20% of patients on thiazide diuretics, dose-dependent
Risk increased in elderly, higher doses, and concurrent potassium-wasting medications
Morning administration recommended to minimize nocturia; afternoon blood tests may show lowest K+ levels

effects.mechanism: Increased distal tubular Na+ delivery stimulates aldosterone, leading to enhanced K+ secretion in cortical collecting duct. Dose-dependent effect.
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check K+ at 1-2 weeks after initiation/start, then every 3-6 months; sooner if symptoms. Consider K+ supplementation if < 3.5 mmol/L.

evidence.text: Thiazide-induced hyponatremia: 2-3% incidence, higher in elderly

effects.mechanism: Impaired urinary diluting capacity leads to water retention. More common in elderly women.
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check Na+ 1-2 weeks after initiation in elderly; symptoms: confusion, nausea, headache

evidence.text: Thiazides reduce urinary calcium excretion by 40-50%

effects.mechanism: Enhanced Ca2+ reabsorption in distal tubule via NCX1/NCKX2 transporters

evidence.text: Can precipitate gout in predisposed individuals

effects.mechanism: Competition for organic anion transporters in proximal tubule, resulting in decreased uric acid secretion
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check uric acid if gout history or symptoms

evidence.text: Dose-dependent effect on glucose metabolism; usually reversible upon discontinuation

effects.mechanism: Impaired insulin secretion (hypokalemia-related) and peripheral glucose utilization. Dose-dependent.
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check fasting glucose at baseline, then annually; more frequently if diabetes risk factors

evidence.text: Small increase in HbA1c (0.1-0.3%) possible in susceptible patients

effects.mechanism: Secondary to glucose elevation; impaired insulin sensitivity

evidence.text: Reversible with dose reduction or discontinuation; concerning if persistent elevation

effects.mechanism: Volume depletion causes prerenal azotemia; usually reversible with hydration or dose reduction
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check creatinine 1-2 weeks after initiation; concerning if increase > 30% from baseline

evidence.text: Reduces SBP by 10-20 mmHg and DBP by 5-10 mmHg

effects.mechanism: Initial: Na+ and volume depletion. Chronic: Vasodilation via unknown mechanism, possibly related to smooth muscle ion channels
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check BP 2-4 weeks after initiation/titration; target < 130/80 mmHg for most patients

evidence.text: Orthostatic hypotension more common in elderly

effects.mechanism: Volume depletion and vasodilation

drug_interactions.management: Contraindicated; 14-day washout required
Avoid or use with caution; monitor for serotonin syndrome
Monitor for bleeding; consider PPI if high GI risk
Monitor INR closely when starting/changing dose
Avoid combination; ECG monitoring if unavoidable

evidence.text: Hyponatremia occurs in 0.5-5% of SSRI users; 10-20% in elderly
Usually develops within 2 weeks of initiation
Most cases resolve within 2 weeks of discontinuation

effects.mechanism: Syndrome of Inappropriate Antidiuretic Hormone (SIADH): SSRI stimulates ADH release → water retention → dilutional hyponatremia
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check Na+ 2-4 weeks after initiation and dose increases in patients >65 or with risk factors; symptoms: confusion, headache, nausea, seizures if severe

evidence.text: 2-3x increased risk of GI bleeding; additive with NSAIDs
Surgical bleeding risk increased; consider stopping 7-14 days pre-surgery

effects.mechanism: Serotonin depletion from platelets → impaired platelet aggregation; SSRIs have antiplatelet effects similar to low-dose aspirin
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Be aware of increased bleeding risk; monitor for bruising, epistaxis, GI bleeding

evidence.text: Average gain 1-5 kg over 6-12 months; variable among individuals
Escitalopram less weight gain than paroxetine, more than fluoxetine

effects.mechanism: Multiple: increased appetite/carbohydrate cravings, improved mood → better eating, metabolic effects; usually gradual over months
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor weight at follow-up visits; consider dietary counseling if significant gain; switching to less weight-promoting agent if problematic

evidence.text: QTc increase 5-10ms at 10mg, 10-15ms at 20mg; Torsades de Pointes very rare
Maximum recommended dose 10mg if >65 years due to QT risk

effects.mechanism: hERG potassium channel blockade → delayed ventricular repolarization; risk increases with dose, especially >20mg
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Avoid in congenital long QT syndrome; avoid concurrent QT-prolonging drugs; ECG monitoring if risk factors present; maximum dose 20mg (10mg if >65 years)

evidence.text: 10-20% in controlled trials; up to 40-50% in practice; often underreported
Escitalopram less sexual side effects than paroxetine

effects.mechanism: Serotonergic effects on sexual response pathways; increased serotonin inhibits dopamine and norepinephrine
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Screen for sexual side effects at follow-up (patients may not volunteer this information); options: dose reduction, drug holiday, adjunctive therapy, or switching to non-SSRI antidepressant
effects.symptom_description: Decreased libido, delayed orgasm, erectile dysfunction, anorgasmia; often dose-dependent; may persist or develop over time

evidence.text: Insomnia 10-15%, somnolence 5-10%; varies by individual

effects.mechanism: Serotonergic effects on sleep architecture; activation vs sedation
effects.monitoring_recommendation: If insomnia: take in morning; if somnolence: take at bedtime; usually improves after 2-4 weeks
effects.symptom_description: Can cause insomnia (especially if taken late) OR somnolence (especially early in treatment); variable between individuals

evidence.text: GI effects in 15-25%; usually resolve within 2 weeks

effects.mechanism: Serotonergic effects on GI motility and sensation
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Usually self-limited; take with food if nausea; consider dose reduction if persistent
effects.symptom_description: Nausea (most common), diarrhea, dyspepsia; usually transient (1-2 weeks)

indications.text: Panic Disorder with or without agoraphobia

monitoring_protocol.frequency: Weekly for first 4 weeks, then periodically

primary_sources.text: FDA Drug Safety Communication: QT prolongation

drug_interactions.management: Monitor BP; may reduce need for prescription meds
Generally safe; monitor for excessive hypotension
Monitor if high-dose arginine with anticoagulants

evidence.text: SBP reduction 2-6 mmHg at 3-6g daily; more in hypertensive patients
Effects modest compared to prescription antihypertensives

effects.mechanism: Substrate for endothelial nitric oxide synthase (eNOS) → increased NO production → vasodilation; effects more pronounced in those with endothelial dysfunction
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor BP if taking antihypertensives; may allow dose reduction of prescription meds

evidence.text: HbA1c reduction 0.1-0.3% in diabetic patients; modest effect

effects.mechanism: Improved insulin sensitivity; enhanced glucose uptake; improved endothelial function in diabetes

evidence.text: Plasma arginine increases dose-dependently; NO metabolites increase 20-50%

effects.mechanism: Direct substrate for NO synthesis; increases plasma arginine

evidence.text: Theoretical risk based on mechanism; limited clinical reports

effects.mechanism: Increased NO may inhibit platelet aggregation; theoretical concern, not well documented
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Caution with anticoagulants/antiplatelets; discontinue 2 weeks before surgery

evidence.text: Based on in vitro data and anecdotal reports; clinical significance uncertain

effects.mechanism: Arginine is essential for herpes virus replication; high arginine may promote viral growth (lysine antagonizes)
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Avoid or reduce dose if history of frequent herpes outbreaks; consider lysine supplementation to antagonize
effects.symptom_description: Reactivation of latent herpes simplex virus; cold sores or genital herpes outbreaks

drug_interactions.management: Avoid or monitor INR closely; significant interaction risk
Use caution; monitor for bleeding
Avoid combination unless specifically advised
Monitor BP

evidence.text: Reduces fibrinogen 5-15% in some studies

effects.mechanism: Fibrinolytic enzyme; degrades fibrin in clots

evidence.text: May increase INR when combined with anticoagulants

effects.mechanism: Fibrinolytic and mild anticoagulant effects
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor INR if on warfarin; discontinue 1-2 weeks before surgery

evidence.text: May prolong aPTT slightly

effects.mechanism: Mild intrinsic pathway effect

evidence.text: Increases fibrin degradation products

effects.mechanism: Increased fibrin degradation from fibrinolytic activity
effects.monitoring_recommendation: May interfere with D-dimer as diagnostic test for thrombosis

evidence.text: Bleeding risk increases with concurrent anticoagulants

effects.mechanism: Fibrinolytic activity; additive with anticoagulants
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Caution with anticoagulants/antiplatelets; discontinue 1-2 weeks before surgery; report unusual bleeding

evidence.text: Small studies show 5-10 mmHg SBP reduction

effects.mechanism: Unknown; possible ACE inhibition (contains bacillopeptidase F)

drug_interactions.management: Monitor INR closely; dose reduction may be needed
Generally safe; caution with high doses of both
Monitor BP; beneficial interaction

evidence.text: 20-30% reduction at 2-4g daily; dose-dependent
Greater effect in patients with baseline TG > 200 mg/dL

effects.mechanism: Multiple: reduced hepatic VLDL synthesis, enhanced TG clearance, activation of PPAR-alpha, inhibition of hepatic lipogenesis
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check lipid panel 8-12 weeks after starting; effects evident at 2-4g EPA+DHA daily

evidence.text: 1-3 mg/dL increase (modest); less clinically significant than TG effect

effects.mechanism: Enhanced reverse cholesterol transport; increased ApoA-I production

evidence.text: LDL may increase 5-10% at high doses (4g); particle size shifts to larger, less atherogenic pattern

effects.mechanism: Increased conversion of VLDL to LDL; shift to larger, less atherogenic particles
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor LDL at 8-12 weeks; consider adding statin if significant rise; particle size shift may be beneficial despite higher numbers

evidence.text: 10-25% reduction in CRP; variable response

effects.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory: reduced pro-inflammatory cytokines, increased resolvins and protectins

evidence.text: Slight prolongation of bleeding time; clinical bleeding rare
No significant bleeding risk in meta-analyses up to 4g daily

effects.mechanism: Incorporation into platelet membranes → reduced TXA2 synthesis → mild antiplatelet effect (less than aspirin)
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Caution with anticoagulants; discontinue 1 week before major surgery; minor increase in bleeding time

evidence.text: INR may increase 0.2-0.5 with high-dose omega-3 and warfarin

effects.mechanism: Mild anticoagulant effect; may potentiate warfarin
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor INR more frequently when starting omega-3; warfarin dose may need 10-20% reduction

evidence.text: SBP reduction 2-4 mmHg; modest effect

effects.mechanism: Endothelial function improvement; NO enhancement; anti-inflammatory effects on vasculature

drug_interactions.management: Contraindicated; use fenofibrate if combination necessary
Contraindicated; use pravastatin if statin needed
Monitor INR frequently when starting/changing dose
Separate administration by 2 hours

evidence.text: Most potent statin on mg-per-mg basis

effects.mechanism: Competitive inhibition of HMG-CoA reductase → decreased hepatic cholesterol synthesis → upregulation of LDL receptors → increased LDL clearance from plasma
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check lipid panel 4-12 weeks after initiation/dose change, then every 3-12 months. LDL goal: <100 mg/dL (or <70 for high risk)

evidence.text: 30-40% reduction in total cholesterol at standard doses

effects.mechanism: Parallel to LDL reduction; also reduces VLDL

evidence.text: 8-14% increase in HDL-C at standard doses

effects.mechanism: Enhanced ApoA-I production; reduced CETP activity; improved reverse cholesterol transport

evidence.text: 10-25% reduction in triglycerides; more pronounced if baseline elevated

effects.mechanism: Reduction in VLDL production; enhanced triglyceride clearance

evidence.text: ALT > 3x ULN in 0.1-1% of patients; usually asymptomatic
Clinically apparent liver injury extremely rare (~1/10,000)

effects.mechanism: Hepatocellular enzyme release; usually asymptomatic and transient; true hepatotoxicity very rare (1 in 10,000)
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check ALT at baseline, 12 weeks after initiation, then annually; discontinue if ALT > 3x ULN on repeat

evidence.text: Parallel ALT/AST elevation pattern

effects.mechanism: Same as ALT elevation

evidence.text: Rhabdomyolysis risk ~1/10,000 patient-years
Myalgia reported in 5-10% of patients (often placebo-equivalent)

effects.mechanism: Muscular enzyme release; myocyte membrane permeability changes; severe elevation (>10x ULN) indicates rhabdomyolysis
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check CK if muscle symptoms (pain, weakness, dark urine); discontinue if CK > 10x ULN without strenuous exercise or if CK > 5x ULN with symptoms

evidence.text: True statin myopathy ~1/1000; myalgia 5-10% (placebo-subtracted ~2-3%)
Often reversible with dose reduction or switching to hydrophilic statin

effects.mechanism: Multiple mechanisms: CoQ10 depletion, altered cell membrane fluidity, reduced mevalonate pathway products; often no CK elevation
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Assess symptom severity; check CK if severe; consider dose reduction, switching statin, or alternate-day dosing; CoQ10 supplementation controversial (may help some patients)
effects.symptom_description: Muscle pain, soreness, weakness, or cramps; usually bilateral and proximal; nocturnal cramps common

evidence.text: 25-35% relative risk reduction in CV events; NNT ~50-100 over 5 years
Benefit proportional to absolute LDL reduction

effects.mechanism: LDL reduction → decreased atherosclerotic plaque progression; pleiotropic effects: improved endothelial function, reduced inflammation, stabilization of atherosclerotic plaques
effects.outcome_metric: Reduction in major cardiovascular events (MI, stroke, CV death)

contraindications.text: Pregnancy ( Category D in 2nd/3rd trimester - fetal toxicity)

drug_interactions.management: Avoid combination or monitor K+ closely
Contraindicated together unless compelling indication with close monitoring
Monitor lithium levels if combination necessary
Monitor renal function and BP
Start low dose; monitor for hypotension

evidence.text: Reduces SBP by 15-20 mmHg and DBP by 10-15 mmHg
Long half-life (24 hours) allows once-daily dosing

effects.mechanism: Selective AT1 receptor antagonism blocks angiotensin II effects: vasoconstriction, aldosterone release, sympathetic activation, and renal sodium reabsorption
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check BP 2-4 weeks after initiation/titration; target < 130/80 mmHg for most patients

evidence.text: Hyperkalemia risk 1-3%, higher with concurrent K+-sparing drugs

effects.mechanism: Reduced aldosterone → decreased distal tubular K+ secretion
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check K+ and creatinine 1-2 weeks after initiation; more frequently if combining with K+-sparing diuretics

evidence.text: Creatinine increase 10-30% is expected; usually stabilizes within 2-4 weeks
Concern if increase > 30% or progressive despite continued therapy

effects.mechanism: Efferent arteriole vasodilation in glomerulus reduces intraglomerular pressure and GFR; this is EXPECTED and usually stabilizes within 2-4 weeks
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check creatinine at baseline, 1-2 weeks, and 1 month; acceptable if increase < 30% from baseline and stabilizes; discontinue if progressive rise or hyperkalemia develops

evidence.text: eGFR decrease 5-15% expected; renal protective long-term

effects.mechanism: Corresponding decrease to creatinine elevation; hemodynamic effect, not true nephrotoxicity

evidence.text: Reduces HbA1c by 0.1-0.3% in diabetic patients; renal protective

effects.mechanism: Improved insulin sensitivity; reduced oxidative stress; renoprotective effects in diabetic nephropathy

evidence.text: Slight reduction in uric acid levels

effects.mechanism: Improved renal perfusion enhances uric acid excretion

evidence.text: First-dose hypotension more common in volume-depleted patients

effects.mechanism: Blood pressure reduction; more common with volume depletion
effects.symptom_description: Dizziness, lightheadedness, especially on standing; fatigue; first-dose effect possible

evidence.text: Angioedema risk ~0.1-0.4%; less than ACE inhibitors
Discontinue immediately if angioedema develops; do not restart

effects.mechanism: Bradykinin-mediated (less common than with ACE inhibitors); can occur even without ACE inhibitor history
effects.symptom_description: Swelling of face, lips, tongue, or throat; potentially life-threatening airway obstruction

contraindications.text: Hereditary optic nerve atrophy (Leber disease) - relative

drug_interactions.management: Monitor B12 annually if on metformin; supplement if needed
Monitor B12 with long-term PPI use
drug_interactions.mechanism: Reduced gastric acid impairs B12 liberation from food

drug_interactions.management: Monitor B12 levels

evidence.text: Reduces homocysteine 15-25% if deficient; less effect if replete

effects.mechanism: Cofactor for methionine synthase (converts homocysteine to methionine)

evidence.text: MMA normalizes with B12 repletion

effects.mechanism: Cofactor for methylmalonyl-CoA mutase

evidence.text: Corrects anemia in deficiency; reticulocytosis in 5-7 days

effects.mechanism: Corrects megaloblastic anemia; essential for DNA synthesis in RBC precursors

evidence.text: MCV normalizes over weeks with treatment

effects.mechanism: Normalization of macrocytosis in deficiency

evidence.text: Serum B12 increases with supplementation

effects.mechanism: Direct supplementation increases serum levels
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Serum B12 may not reflect tissue status; check MMA if concerns

monitoring_protocol.frequency: Annually if deficient or at risk

drug_interactions.management: Monitor CK; increased risk with combination
Beneficial - take aspirin 30min before niacin
Monitor BP

evidence.text: HDL increase 15-35% at therapeutic doses (1-3g)

effects.mechanism: Inhibits hepatic HDL clearance; increases ApoA-I synthesis

evidence.text: TG reduction 20-30%

effects.mechanism: Inhibits hepatic VLDL synthesis

evidence.text: LDL reduction 10-25%

effects.mechanism: Reduces VLDL production and LDL formation

evidence.text: Can precipitate gout in susceptible individuals

effects.mechanism: Competes with uric acid for renal excretion
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check uric acid if history of gout

evidence.text: Glucose may increase 5-10% at high doses

effects.mechanism: Insulin resistance induction at high doses
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor glucose if diabetes or prediabetes

evidence.text: Flush occurs in 70%+ at therapeutic doses; not toxic

effects.mechanism: Prostaglandin release from skin cells; vasodilation
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Take with food; aspirin 325mg 30min before can reduce; extended-release reduces flush
effects.symptom_description: Cutaneous flushing, warmth, itching; prostaglandin-mediated; tolerance develops

evidence.text: ALT/AST elevation >3x ULN in 0.5-3%

effects.mechanism: Hepatic steatosis at high doses; dose-dependent
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Check LFTs at baseline and during therapy

drug_interactions.management: Avoid concurrent use unless carbidopa included
drug_interactions.mechanism: B6 enhances peripheral decarboxylation of levodopa

drug_interactions.management: B6 supplementation recommended with isoniazid (25-50mg)

evidence.text: Reduces homocysteine 10-20% when deficient

effects.mechanism: Cofactor for homocysteine metabolism (cystathionine beta-synthase)

evidence.text: Neurotoxicity at doses >100mg/day; usually reversible

effects.mechanism: Toxic metabolite accumulation with chronic high-dose use
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Do not exceed 100mg/day long-term; caution >50mg/day
effects.symptom_description: Sensory neuropathy: numbness, ataxia, burning; dose-dependent; reversible if caught early

drug_interactions.management: Monitor calcium levels; usually beneficial for bones
Take vitamin D at different time; monitor levels
May need higher vitamin D doses

evidence.text: Calcium increases 0.1-0.3 mg/dL with repletion; hypercalcemia rare at standard doses

effects.mechanism: Vitamin D enhances intestinal calcium absorption (active transport); increases renal calcium reabsorption; mobilizes bone calcium
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor calcium if taking high doses (>4000 IU) or if history of hypercalcemia; check at 8-12 weeks

evidence.text: PTH decreases 15-30% with vitamin D repletion; beneficial for bone health

effects.mechanism: Negative feedback: increased calcium and direct vitamin D effects on parathyroid gland suppress PTH secretion

evidence.text: Rare case reports of hyperuricemia with high-dose vitamin D

effects.mechanism: Increased calcium may reduce uric acid excretion; competition for renal tubular transport

evidence.text: Slight increase in stone risk with high-dose calcium/vitamin D; standard doses likely safe

effects.mechanism: Hypercalciuria from increased calcium absorption; urinary calcium excretion increases
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Use caution with high doses (>4000 IU) in stone formers; ensure adequate hydration; consider lower doses

drug_interactions.management: Avoid unless specifically prescribed; if used, keep dose consistent and monitor INR
Beneficial combination for bone health
drug_interactions.mechanism: K2 directs calcium, D3 increases absorption

evidence.text: Vitamin K antagonizes warfarin; INR decreases with supplementation
MK-7 has longer half-life than vitamin K1; more stable effect

effects.mechanism: Cofactor for gamma-carboxylation of clotting factors II, VII, IX, X; antagonizes warfarin effect
effects.monitoring_recommendation: AVOID with warfarin unless specifically prescribed; if used together, monitor INR frequently; vitamin K intake should be consistent

evidence.text: Increases functional vitamin K-dependent clotting factors

effects.mechanism: Gamma-carboxylation activation of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors

evidence.text: Increases carboxylated osteocalcin; improves bone density

effects.mechanism: Carboxylation of osteocalcin → improved bone mineral binding; directs calcium to bone

evidence.text: Reduces inactive ucMGP; may slow vascular calcification

effects.mechanism: Carboxylation of MGP → calcium binding → inhibition of vascular calcification

drug_interactions.management: Separate by 2-4 hours
Separate by 2-4 hours
Separate by 2 hours
May need slightly higher zinc intake
Take at different times; ratio 10-15:1 zinc:copper

evidence.text: Copper deficiency can occur with chronic high-dose zinc

effects.mechanism: High-dose zinc induces metallothionein, which binds copper and increases its excretion
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Monitor copper if taking >50mg zinc long-term; supplement ratio 10-15:1 (zinc:copper)

evidence.text: HDL may decrease with very high zinc doses

effects.mechanism: Unknown; may be related to copper deficiency or direct effect

evidence.text: GI upset common if taken on empty stomach

effects.mechanism: Irritation of gastric mucosa; zinc ion effects
effects.monitoring_recommendation: Take with food if nausea occurs
effects.symptom_description: Nausea, stomach upset, metallic taste; take with food to reduce

evidence.text: Restores immune function in deficiency; benefits in common cold unclear

effects.mechanism: Essential for immune cell function; deficiency impairs immunity
